rationales.achievement: She spends her weekend building the classroom herself, mapping the first unit, and treating a strong start as essential to the year's success.
rationales.benevolence: She pays for materials out of pocket and sets up the room because she wants her students to keep their reading momentum from the start.

rationales.self_direction: She chooses Birch Lane because it is the program she believed in, rejecting the safer district role in favor of the work she actually wants to do.
rationales.security: Turning down the offer with better benefits, class caps, and predictability is an explicit move away from the safer option.
rationales.benevolence: Her decision is anchored in the students who have started trusting her, especially Mateo and the other children in her class.

rationales.self_direction: Even after a sleepless night, she still cannot bring herself to reverse course, reinforcing that she is sticking with the path she chose.
rationales.security: She explicitly names the salary and loan relief she would gain, then still refuses to take that more financially secure option.

rationales.self_direction: She deletes the stable district job emails because she cannot picture herself teaching from someone else's curriculum.
rationales.security: She keeps rejecting steady pay, a pension, and proximity to family even while new-hire layoffs are being discussed.

rationales.benevolence: The part that matters most to her is Mateo reading to his little brother after she sends books home, showing clear investment in her students' wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: She contrasts this work with student teaching in Edina, where she only followed someone else's plan; here she feels she is building something herself.
rationales.achievement: She tracks concrete reading gains and stays late reorganizing the library baskets so the system works better for the class.
rationales.benevolence: Her extra effort is aimed at helping each child find books at the right level and helping the lower group catch up.

rationales.self_direction: After running the numbers, she still insists that her life should not be organized around what other people think should matter most.
rationales.security: She explicitly notes the extra nine hundred dollars a month she is forgoing, which makes this a clear rejection of the safer financial path.

rationales.self_direction: She frames the choice as building a life around something she actually chose rather than around fear or other people's calculations.
rationales.security: She knowingly rejects the path with better benefits, lower downside, and a clearer safety net.
rationales.benevolence: When asked what she chose at Birch Lane, her answer is the kids, which centers the welfare of the children she teaches.

rationales.self_direction: Even after the budget scare eases, she reaffirms that she is staying because this is still the choice she keeps making for herself.
rationales.security: She can list the safer reasons on paper but still chooses the less stable school, so the entry remains clearly misaligned with Security.
